title: Craft Workflow Definition Schema (constrained dialect, v1)
description: Reference dialect for WORKFLOW-*.yaml definitions consumed by /craft:orchestrate:workflow. Documented for reference only; enforcement is the deterministic stdlib structural validator in scripts/workflow_parse.py (D2). v1 dialect is DELIBERATELY constrained — required keys, primitive types, and homogeneous arrays only. NO oneOf, regex pattern, or conditional subschemas, so the stdlib validator never needs jsonschema.

inputs.description: Named workflow inputs. Each value is a primitive type spec with optional required flag.

max_concurrent.description: Run-wide semaphore ceiling: max simultaneously-live agents across all waves (D5/FR7). Defaults to 16 if omitted. A per-parallel-stage max_concurrent caps that wave's width and may not exceed this ceiling.

stages.description: Ordered pipeline stages. Control flow is fixed in the definition; only parallel fan-out volume flexes to upstream data.

id.description: Unique stage identifier, referenced by downstream ${id.field} bindings

agent.description: Per-fan-out agent spec for type=parallel stages

over.description: Data-driven fan-out binding for type=parallel: ${stage.field} (one array) or ${stage.*.field} (flatten one level across the upstream collection). Empty resolved array is a HARD ERROR naming the upstream stage (D6/FR8).

fan.description: Replicas per bound element (e.g. fan: 2 = 2 verifiers per finding). Default 1.

max_concurrent.description: Per-stage wave-width cap; must not exceed the run-wide ceiling

input.description: Input binding for the stage: ${stage.field} or ${stage.*} (all outputs of an upstream stage)

command.description: For type=verify (D8): a real project command whose exit status is the authoritative pass/fail. A green transcript is not sufficient.

output_schema.description: Constrained structural contract for an agent's JSON output (D2 dialect). Shorthand form: each key maps to a type spec string. All declared keys are required.
